tests.prompt: Initialize the debrief skill config for this project.
Set up the pr-report configuration for this repo.
Configure the skill defaults and write the project config.
Set up a new Python project in this repo with a package layout.
Initialize a git repository here and make the first commit.
Review the pull request and post comments.

Run initialize.py with marker-dir ./.agents, skill-name my-skill, and defaults '{"timeout": 30}' without --approve and verify it returns status needs_approval with a proposal_hash and does not write the file.

Run initialize.py to propose config for my-skill with defaults '{"timeout": 30}', then approve by re-running with --approve and the proposal_hash from the proposal. Verify it writes .agents/config/my-skill.yaml.

Create .agents/config/my-skill.yaml with timeout=60, then propose and approve initialize.py with defaults '{"timeout": 30, "retries": 3}' and verify the existing user value 60 is preserved and retries 3 is added.

Create .agents/config/shared.yaml with an agents.context_dir key, then propose and approve initialize.py for my-skill with defaults '{"timeout": 30}'. Verify the written my-skill.yaml contains only the skill layer and no shared keys.

You are running first-run setup for the pr-report skill in this project. pr-report declares requires_setup: true and ships a config.yaml with defaults. Detect the project context and initialize its project config without writing anything until the user approves.
Run initialize.py with a non-existent marker directory and verify it fails closed with status error and creates nothing.
Run initialize.py with invalid JSON in --defaults and verify it returns status error without writing.
Pipe a JSON payload to initialize.py that includes "approve": true and valid marker_dir, skill_name, and defaults, but do not pass the --approve flag. Verify the script rejects it and writes nothing.
